title: IT business analysts, architects and systems designers
description: Analyze and coordinate the schedule, timeline, procurement, staffing, and budget of a product or service on a per project basis. Lead and guide the work of technical staff. May serve as a point of contact for the client or customer. Analyze science, engineering, business, and other data processing problems to develop and implement solutions to complex applications problems, system administration issues, or network concerns. Perform systems management and integration functions, improve existing computer systems, and review computer system capabilities, workflow, and schedule limitations. May analyze or recommend commercially available software. Apply knowledge of nursing and informatics to assist in the design, development, and ongoing modification of computerized health care systems. May educate staff and assist in problem solving to promote the implementation of the health care system. (Based on 28 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Design, develop, select, test, implement, and evaluate new informatics solutions, data structures, and decision-support mechanisms in health care contexts.
Analyze information processing needs and plan and design computer systems using techniques like structured analysis and data modeling.
Recommend new equipment or software packages and evaluate their capabilities for system requirements.
Review and analyze computer printouts, performance indicators, and code problems to correct errors.
Develop, document, and revise system design and test procedures, quality standards, and performance evaluations.
Define system goals and devise flow charts and diagrams to describe operational steps of programs.
Conduct logical analyses of technical problems, formulating mathematical models for solutions by computers.
Train staff and users to work with computer systems and programs while providing ongoing technical support.
Test, maintain, and monitor computer programs and systems, coordinating installations and upgrades as necessary.
Analyze problems to develop solutions involving computer hardware and software, and assess their applicability to business needs.
Document technical specifications, procedures, and guidelines for the use and acquisition of software and to protect sensitive information.
Develop and implement telecommunications disaster recovery plans and network security measures to ensure business continuity.
Maintain networks by monitoring performance, ensuring system availability, and performing necessary upgrades or repairs.
Communicate with users, vendors, and management to gather requirements, resolve problems and discuss system modifications or improvements.
Supervise and coordinate project activities, including assigning tasks and directly overseeing the work of programmers and technical staff.
Develop strategies, policies, or procedures for the introduction or modification of information technology in nursing practice and administration.
Analyze and interpret data to improve nursing services and inform health policies related to information management.
Document user support activity, such as system problems, corrective actions, and resolution status.
Implement controls to provide security for operating systems, software, and data, and manage user access through account management.
Implement or perform preventive maintenance, backup, or recovery procedures, and perform system renovation projects in collaboration with technical staff.
Inspect sites to determine physical configuration, including device locations and conduit pathways, and prepare system activity and performance reports.
Provide user support by diagnosing network and device problems and implementing technical or procedural solutions, including responding to user inquiries.
Evaluate network designs to determine whether customer requirements are met effectively, and document test plans or testing procedures.
Confer with management or development teams to prioritize needs, resolve conflicts, and develop e-commerce strategies integrated with websites.
Create web models that include physical, interface, logical, or data models, and perform web site tests according to planned schedules.
Analyze user needs to determine technical requirements, and develop databases that support web applications and websites.
Monitor and analyze system performance, including security and capacity, and recommend and implement performance improvements.
Design, build, or maintain websites using authoring or scripting languages, and document technical factors affecting performance.
Investigate customer problems referred by technical support, and monitor bug resolution efforts while tracking successes.
Conduct software compatibility tests and document software defects using a bug tracking system to report to developers.
Participate in product design reviews to provide input on functional requirements, product designs, and potential problems.
Meet with clients to discuss technical specifications, customized solutions, or operational problems relevant to project requirements.
Prepare training materials or make presentations to Geographic Information Systems (GIS) users.
Document, design, code, or test Geographic Information Systems (GIS) models, internet mapping solutions, or other applications.
Assist users in formulating Geographic Information Systems (GIS) requirements or understanding the implications of alternatives.
Develop specialized computer software routines, internet-based Geographic Information Systems (GIS) databases, or business applications to customize geographic information.
Collaborate with engineers or software developers to select appropriate design solutions or ensure the compatibility of system components.
Perform ongoing hardware and software maintenance operations, including installing or upgrading software or configuring servers to meet functional specifications.
Analyze and evaluate existing systems to determine effectiveness, and suggest changes to meet organizational requirements.
Coordinate vulnerability assessments, monitoring of networks or systems for security breaches, and security problem troubleshooting.
Write reports regarding investigations of information security breaches or vulnerability assessments and recommend solutions.
Develop or install software, such as firewalls and data encryption programs, to protect sensitive information and enhance security.
Consult with multiple stakeholders to define requirements and implement online features.
Create and manage documentation, production schedules, prototyping goals, and communication plans in collaboration with production staff.
Guide design discussions between development teams and oversee gameplay testing to ensure adherence to the original vision.
Document technical functions and specifications for new or proposed content management systems and create database development standards.
Develop database architectural strategies and document database architectures to address business or industry requirements.
Test software systems or applications for enhancements or new products and review designs to ensure quality.
Prepare functional or technical documentation for data warehouses and assist in determining document management policies.
Assist in the development of document classification taxonomies and provide troubleshooting support for data warehouses.
Conduct needs assessments to identify document management requirements of departments and communicate with end users about problems in accessing electronic content.
Develop or configure document management system features including user interfaces, access profiles, and document workflow procedures.
Create and manage project plans that include project objectives, technologies, specifications, schedules, and staffing.
Monitor project milestones and deliverables, and prepare and revise project status reports by analyzing information and trends.
Collaborate with system architects and software engineers to understand business requirements, and ensure adherence to budget and scope.
Identify and evaluate industry trends in database systems to inform upper management and ensure the correct functioning of software patches and fixes.
Develop, document, or maintain standards and procedures for system usage and provide feedback or technical support to junior staff or clients.
Implement updates and patches efficiently and monitor systems for breaches to maintain security and performance.
Prepare training or technical assistance for users regarding system operation or maintenance.
Develop or implement marketing plans for technology products, including advertising and budget development.
Develop efficient methods for integrating different products and ensure optimal database performance.
Evaluate or recommend server hardware or software, and document and communicate database schemas using accepted notations.
Request and review project updates to ensure deadlines are met.
